name: DecisionGraph
description: A small local-first library for keeping AI decisions replayable. Useful when someone asks, later, 'why did the system do that?' and hand-waving is not enough.

name: SchemaPilot
description: A data-quality control layer for AI datasets. It keeps schemas, access rules, and pipeline steps boringly explicit before anyone tries to put a model on top.

name: PECR
description: A retrieval runtime with policy checks built in. The point is simple: if the model cannot see it, it should not pretend it did.

name: retokenization-invariance-atlas
description: A test harness for checking whether tiny prompt-format changes break QA behavior. Because if a newline changes the answer, you probably want to know before launch.

name: ScrubID
description: An audit pipeline for interpretability claims. It asks whether the evidence actually identifies what people say it identifies, not just whether the chart looks convincing.

name: certipatch
description: A repair loop for frozen language models. It patches failing behaviors and leaves behind replayable evidence instead of a hopeful note in a changelog.

name: discover-then-distill-paper
description: A research implementation that separates quick adaptation from durable model improvement. Basically: did the model learn something, or did it just cope for a moment?

name: cost-watchdog
description: A self-hosted cost monitor that catches weird spend before the monthly report ruins someone's morning.

name: VeilPack
description: An offline privacy gate for data pipelines. It catches sensitive values, redacts them, and packages the result so teams can prove what happened.

name: ContextGraph
description: A privacy-aware process map built from Slack, Jira, and GitHub metadata. It tries to show where work gets stuck without turning people into surveillance objects.

name: incitape
description: A telemetry tape for replaying incidents. Less folklore in postmortems, more 'here is what actually happened'.

name: AvalancheLLM
description: A reproducibility pack for studying activation cascades in LLMs. It looks for instability patterns before they become mysterious production behavior.

name: Veil
description: A local PII detection and redaction toolkit for text and documents. For cases where sending sensitive content to yet another cloud tool is exactly the wrong move.

name: egress-lockfile
description: A versioned lockfile for outbound network access. Boring on purpose: make egress explicit, reviewable, and hard to quietly drift.

name: AgentGate
description: A policy and approval gateway for AI agents. Agents can move fast; this gives teams somewhere sane to put the brakes.

name: AnnexOps
description: Evidence packs as code for EU AI Act-style compliance work. Less spreadsheet archaeology, more versioned proof.

name: flakeguard
description: A CI flake detector for GitHub Actions. It helps separate real breakage from tests that just enjoy wasting everyone's time.

name: BatteryPassportCompiler
description: A deterministic compiler for Battery Passport artifacts. It turns compliance data into signed, replayable proof objects.

name: cis-technical-report
description: The written version of my reliability approach: deterministic checks, evidence boundaries, and the parts teams should decide before production pressure hits.

name: cachemedicpp-research
description: A research package for breaking and repairing KV-cache behavior in a controlled way. If the serving layer fails, I want the failure to be reproducible.

name: intake-routing-engine
description: A self-hosted email intake router. It extracts the facts, applies explicit rules, and avoids turning every incoming request into a vibe check.
